## Meeting Identity

**Meeting ID:** 2026-01-29_Chris-Hanson_360-EF-Coaching
**Date:** 2026-01-29
**Duration:** ~30 minutes
**Format:** Video call
**Attendees:** Vrijen Attawar, Chris Hanson

## Stakeholder Classification

**Primary:** Founder / EdTech entrepreneur — Chris Hanson is the founder of 360 (executive function coaching platform targeting K-12), a former special education teacher turned small business owner building an AI-augmented coaching product.

**Secondary:** Potential network peer / EdTech collaborator — shared domain overlap with Careerspan in coaching-adjacent tech, non-technical founder navigating product-market fit.

## Key Themes

1. **Executive function coaching as a product** — Chris built 360, an EF coaching platform over ~1 year, targeting individuals ($10/mo), supporters ($20/mo for 10 profiles), and organizations (custom quotes)
2. **Non-technical founder challenges** — Both V and Chris navigating tech product development without engineering backgrounds; reliance on generalist technical help (Joe Coleman)
3. **Go-to-market strategy for EdTech** — V advised on avoiding pilot death spirals, finding a linchpin design partner, keeping feature scope tight, and prioritizing paid pilots over free ones
4. **Product-market fit and niche selection** — V shared Careerspan's journey of narrowing ICP; advised Chris that "every time you think you're too narrow, you're still too broad"
5. **Community and authentic brand building** — Discussion of building in public, leveraging Chris's 65K email list, user interest groups, and authenticity on social media as a founder who works with kids
6. **FOHI introduction** — V offered to connect Chris to the Future of Higher Education community (6K members globally, V is NY co-lead) as a source of EdTech leads and network
